titleMn: Өдөр 28: 4-р долоо хоногийн давталт ба Аялал
objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 travel words, mixed tenses review (past/present/future), listening, speaking, homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. 20 шинэ үг, бүх цагийн давталт (өнгөрсөн/одоо/ирээдүй), сонсох, ярих, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Review past, present, and future tenses together while learning travel vocabulary.
contentMn: Аяллын үгсийг сурахын зэрэгцээ өнгөрсөн, одоо, ирээдүй цагийг хольж давтана.

vocabulary.example: I need my passport to travel.
She applied for a visa.
The hotel was very comfortable.
I packed my luggage last night.
My flight is at 6 AM.
We arrived at the airport early.
I checked in at the hotel.
We took a tour of the city.
I bought souvenirs for my family.
I changed my currency at the bank.
I used a map to find the way.
The guide was very helpful.
We went sightseeing all day.
Traveling is a great adventure.
What is your destination?
I made a hotel booking online.
The flight had a delay.
Go to Gate 5 for your flight.
Boarding starts at 6:30.
When are you going to return?

quiz.question: Now I _____ English.
quiz.explanation: Present Continuous for actions happening now.

quiz.question: Next year I _____ to travel.
quiz.explanation: Going to + base verb expresses future plans.

quiz.question: I _____ to school by bus every day.
What did Bat do last year?
quiz.explanation: Bat said 'Last year, I went to a concert.'

quiz.question: What is Sarnai going to do next summer?
quiz.explanation: She is going to have her first art exhibition.

quiz.question: What tense is 'I am going to study'?
quiz.explanation: am/is/are + going to + base verb = future plan.

quiz.question: _____ you travel often?
quiz.explanation: Habit questions use 'Do/Does + subject + base verb?'

quiz.question: Last week it _____ very cold.
quiz.explanation: Past tense of 'is' for singular 'it' is 'was'.

page1.grammarTopic: Mixed tenses review — Past / Present / Future together
page1.grammarExplanation: In real conversations, we mix ALL tenses together. PAST tells what happened: 'I went to Korea last year.' PRESENT describes now: 'I am studying English now.' FUTURE shows plans: 'I am going to travel next summer.' Key signal words: PAST → yesterday/last year/ago. PRESENT → now/every day. FUTURE → tomorrow/next week/going to. · Өдөр тутмын яриандаа бид БҮХ цагийг хольж хэрэглэдэг. ӨНГӨРСӨН — yesterday/last year. ОДОО — now/every day. ИРЭЭДҮЙ — tomorrow/next week/going to.

grammarTable.subject: Going to
grammarTable.negative: I am not going to stay.
grammarTable.question: Are you going to go?

quickPractice: 1. Yesterday I _____ to the park. (go — past)
4. I _____ to school by bus every day. (go — habit)
7. Last week it _____ very cold. (be — past)
commonMistakes: Yesterday I go to school. → Yesterday I WENT to school.
Now I study. → Now I AM STUDYING.
I am going to went. → I am going to GO.
Last year I am happy. → Last year I WAS happy.

page2.readingPassage: Bat loves travel. Last year, he visited his uncle in the countryside. It was an amazing adventure. Right now, he is saving money every month. He studies English every day because he knows it is important for travel. Next summer, he is going to travel to South Korea with Sarnai. They are going to visit museums, eat great food, and practice their English every day!

keyPhrases.mongolian: Би өдөр бүр англи хэл судалдаг

keyPhrases.english: Next year I am going to travel
keyPhrases.mongolian: Ирэх жил би аялах гэж байна

speakingQuestions: 1. Have you ever traveled outside Mongolia? / Та Монголоос гадна аялж байсан уу?
2. Where did you go last holiday?
3. Where are you going to travel in the future?
4. What country would you like to visit?
5. What did you do during your last vacation?
6. Are you going to travel this summer?

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Did you go anywhere interesting last year?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes! I went to visit my relatives in the countryside.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: We rode horses and saw beautiful mountains.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: That sounds amazing! Where are you going now?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I am going to the library to study for my exam.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: What are you going to do this summer?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I am going to travel to South Korea! English will really help me!

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Teacher: Today we review all tenses. Bat, tell us about your past, present, and future! Bat: OK! Last year, I went to a concert. Right now, I am studying English at school. Next year, I am going to study at university! Teacher: Perfect use of all three tenses! Sarnai, your turn! Sarnai: Last month, I won an art competition. Now, I am working on a new painting. Next summer, I am going to have my first art exhibition! Teacher: Excellent! You both used past, present, and future perfectly. That is what real English sounds like! Students: Thank you, teacher! We love learning English!

listeningQuestions.question: What did Bat do last year?
What is Bat going to do next year?
What did Sarnai do last month?
What is Sarnai going to do next summer?
What does the teacher say about their English?
What tense is used for 'last year I went'?
What tense is 'I am going to study'?

grammarPractice: 3. Next year I _____ to travel. (going to)
4. I _____ to school by bus every day. (go-habit)
homework: Writing: Write 3 paragraphs — past (what you did last week), present (what you do every day), future (what you are going to do).
Vocabulary: Review all 20 travel words and use 5 in sentences.
Speaking: Tell a friend in English: one past event, one present habit, and one future plan.
Review: Past = yesterday/last · Present = now/every day · Future = tomorrow/going to.
answerKey: Fill in blanks: 1-went · 2-am studying · 3-am going · 4-go · 5-was · 6-am going to
